what_it_does: A ledger-style interpretive summary of a design-corpus family-study note (vt-family.md). It records what the study observed, what it confirmed about the design system's surface axis, LOD ladder, and template parameterisation, what atoms it harvested, and how it updated a requirements register.
observations: It documents a family study of five print/one-pager artifacts: vt-architects (4pp A4), vt-gatehouse (3pp), vt-property (2pp), vt-residential (2pp), and vi-onepager (1pp 16:9).
It asserts the design-system grammar spans slide (16:9, 3:2), web scroll, and A4 print without a grammar change.
It asserts an LOD ladder from summary (1pp) through terse-pitch (16pp), standard (17pp), to high-detail (49pp).
It flags vt-gatehouse/p-1 and vt-residential/p-1 as the same one-pager template filled with different client and prose, treating audience/client as a template parameter.
It identifies a new atom: a stage/progress chevron stepper whose accent slides along the gold-to-bronze ramp per variant.
It notes other reusable atoms: a comparison table, section bands, an annotation callout/pointer, and a two-pane one-pager archetype.
It uses status markers (✅, ✏️, ➕, ⚠️) and ends with a '→ Docs updated' provenance line listing register changes.
It references requirement ids (G3, C2, D2) and artifact pages (vt-gatehouse/p-1, vt-residential/p-1).
It declares an empty symbols array and contains no code symbols.
standouts: The empirical claim that the gold-to-bronze ramp position is a per-variant theming parameter, shown by the chevron stepper recoloring per client.
The cross-surface claim that audience/client works as a template parameter across decks, web, and now print.
The file functions as a confirmation/no-contradiction study that strengthens existing hypotheses rather than introducing new grammar.
conventions: Frontmatter-style header block with Sources, Status, and Verdict fields.
Use of ★ to flag high-signal findings.
Use of emoji markers (✅, ✏️, ➕, ⚠️) in a diff-style section.
Artifact and requirement references by id and page (vt-gatehouse/p-1, G3, C2, D2).
Closing '→ Docs updated' provenance line naming which registry docs changed.
JSON schema with fields for observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, themes, intention, novelty, contribution, and embedding summaries.
concerns: The LOD page counts (1/16/17/49pp) are stated without showing whether they were measured or estimated.
The source artifacts and requirements register are referenced but not included or linked inside this file.
The symbols array is empty even though the note describes several design atoms.
The verdict 'No new grammar' sits alongside newly listed atoms (stepper, annotation callout, two-pane one-pager), creating a local tension about what counts as grammar versus atom.
notes: A metadata/interpretive record that packages a family-study analysis into a structured ledger entry, confirming cross-surface template parameterisation and harvesting a few reusable atoms.
questions: Where are the source artifacts (vt-architects, vt-gatehouse, vt-property, vt-residential, vi-onepager) stored and in what format?
Where is the requirements register (G3, C2, D2) and what format does it use?
Were the LOD page counts measured from the actual artifacts or estimated?
How does this ledger entry relate to the original vt-family.md source and any downstream generated docs?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the file states it is a family-study confirmation note and delivers structured observations, standouts, and registry updates consistent with that purpose.
apparent_themes: Template plus parameter (content and audience as data)
One grammar across surfaces and LODs
Tokens/ramp as a parametric theming axis
intention_signals: To create a machine-readable interpretive record of a design-corpus study so a fused system can ingest its confirmed hypotheses, reusable atoms, and provenance without re-reading the original markdown.
novelty: It treats a design-analysis markdown note as a ledger substrate, encoding its empirical claims, signal markers, and registry impact into a structured JSON entry with embedding-ready summaries.
contribution: Contributes a structured interpretation of the vt-family study, capturing its cross-surface confirmation, template-parameter insight, ramp-theming finding, and harvested atoms, plus its convention of linking analysis conclusions to register updates.
summary_for_embedding: Structured interpretive ledger entry for a design-corpus family study confirming A4-print/summary-LOD coverage, audience-as-parameter, and gold-ramp variant theming, with harvested atoms and registry-update provenance.
intention_for_embedding: To encode the findings and provenance of a design-system family study so it can be fused into a broader substrate ledger.
